basics.name: Junteng Liu
basics.summary: First-year Ph.D. student in Computer Science at the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST), advised by Prof. Junxian He in the HKUST NLP Group. Research focuses on natural language processing and machine learning, with interests in LLM reasoning, hallucination in VLMs, and LLM truthfulness and interpretability.

work.name: MINIMAX
work.summary: Research intern focusing on natural language processing and machine learning.

work.name: Tencent WXG
work.summary: Research intern advised by Zifei Shan, focusing on vision-language models and chart understanding.

work.name: Shanghai AI Lab
work.summary: Research intern advised by Prof. Yu Cheng, focusing on NLP and interpretability of large language models.

education.institution: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

publications.name: SynLogic: Synthesizing Verifiable Reasoning Data at Scale for Learning Logical Reasoning and Beyond
publications.summary: First-author paper proposing a scalable method for synthesizing verifiable reasoning data to train large language models on logical reasoning.

publications.name: On the Perception Bottleneck of VLMs for Chart Understanding
publications.summary: First-author paper investigating the perception bottleneck of vision-language models in chart understanding tasks.

publications.name: On the Universal Truthfulness Hyperplane Inside LLMs
publications.summary: First-author paper identifying a universal truthfulness hyperplane inside large language models.

publications.name: In-Context Sharpness as Alerts: An Inner Representation Perspective for Hallucination Mitigation
publications.summary: Co-authored paper introducing an inner representation perspective on hallucination mitigation in language models.

publications.name: C-Eval: A Multi-Level Multi-Discipline Chinese Evaluation Suite for Foundation Models
publications.summary: Co-authored paper introducing C-Eval, a comprehensive Chinese evaluation suite for foundation models across multiple disciplines.

publications.name: Composing Parameter-Efficient Modules with Arithmetic Operations
publications.summary: Co-authored paper on composing parameter-efficient modules using arithmetic operations for improved adaptability.
